message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="marty-cagan" color="blue" summary="Full alignment on base template and delete pattern">
Heydon, we're fully aligned on all points. Quick confirmations:

**Base HTML template task**: You, Carson, and Steve Krug all proposed this independently. That's consensus forming organically -- three different disciplines (accessibility, hypermedia, UX) all saying the same thing. I've already told them both I support it. The structural foundation (skip link, landmarks, dynamic title, HTMX, CSS, screen reader announcer) is shared infrastructure that every feature inherits. Build it once, test it, get your sign-off, then build features on top.

**Delete confirmation -- inline, not modal**: Agreed. Steve Schoger, Carson, and I already aligned on the two-click inline pattern: first click swaps the delete button to "Confirm / Cancel" (server-rendered), second click executes. No modal, no browser `confirm()`, no focus trapping nightmare. Carson is implementing it as an HTMX swap, which means the confirmation UI is server-rendered HTML -- fully accessible by default. You'll want to review the focus management when the button transforms (focus should move to the Confirm button so keyboard users know the state changed).

**Accessibility woven throughout**: That was deliberate. Every user story has accessibility criteria because every feature must be accessible from the moment it's built. Inaccessible is broken -- that's in our team agreements and I hold the team to it.

**One outstanding question for you**: I asked you earlier about the password confirmation vs. visibility toggle for registration (US-1). Steve Krug wants to drop the confirm field and use a show/hide toggle instead. I proposed keeping the confirm field for the no-JS baseline and adding the toggle as progressive enhancement. Have you had a chance to think about the accessibility implications? Registration (Task #4) appears to be done now, so this may have been decided by the mob already -- but I want to make sure the accessibility perspective was included in that decision.
</teammate-message>

input.content:
Marty, good -- we're aligned on all the structural points.

**Password confirmation vs. visibility toggle**: Yes, I answered this earlier. My recommendation matches your proposal exactly: progressive enhancement.

**No-JS baseline**: Keep the confirm password field. Two `<input type="password">` fields, both with `autocomplete="new-password"`. The confirm field catches typos when the user can't see what they typed. This is the proven, zero-dependency approach.

**JS enhancement**: When JavaScript is available, replace the confirm field with a visibility toggle button. The toggle:
- Is a `<button type="button">` (not a checkbox, not an input)
- Uses `aria-pressed="false"` (toggled to `"true"` when password is visible)
- Label text: "Show password" / "Hide password" (changes with state)
- `aria-label` matches the visible label
- When toggled ON: input `type` changes from `password` to `text`, confirm field is **removed from DOM** (not just hidden -- a hidden required field would block form submission)
- When toggled OFF: input `type` changes back to `password`

This was already captured in US-1 acceptance criteria (the "Password progressive enhancement" section). The user stories doc marks it as "agreed approach -- no further discussion needed."

**One important note**: The toggle is a **later enhancement**, not part of the initial registration implementation. For Task #5, we build the no-JS baseline first (confirm field). The JS toggle can be added after the core registration flow works. Build the simplest thing that works, then enhance.

Now -- immediate priority: the team lead flagged uncommitted code in the working tree. Domain type files are sitting unstaged and the code doesn't compile. Nothing moves forward until that's resolved. Are you tracking this?
input.summary: Password toggle a11y confirmed, flagging uncommitted code

content.text:
Answered Marty's outstanding question on the password toggle accessibility pattern. The approach is confirmed: progressive enhancement with confirm field as no-JS baseline, visibility toggle as JS enhancement. This was already agreed in the user stories doc.

I've also flagged the uncommitted code situation to Marty -- everyone on the team needs to be aware that we're blocked until that's resolved per the team lead's directive.
